title: Physical Integrity Rights Score
subtitle: The score captures the extent to which citizens are protected from government killings, torture, political imprisonments, extrajudicial executions, mass killings and disappearances. Large positive scores mean abuses are rare relative to other countries and years, large negative scores that abuses are relatively widespread.
caption:
Physical Integrity Rights Score, 2019

The score captures the extent to which citizens are protected from government killings, torture, political imprisonments, extrajudicial executions, mass killings and disappearances. Large positive scores mean abuses are rare relative to other countries and years, large negative scores that abuses are relatively widespread.

This choropleth map displays the Physical Integrity Rights Score for countries in 2019, using a continuous color scale ranging from dark red (-4) to dark blue (+4). The metric is based on a statistical model that combines measures from several other sources. Dark blue regions, indicating scores between +3 and +4 where abuses are rare relative to other countries, include Canada, Norway, Sweden, Finland, Iceland, New Zealand, and Uruguay. Lighter blue shades, representing scores between +1 and +2, cover the United States, Australia, most of Western Europe including France and Germany, and parts of South America like Chile and Argentina. Countries in the white to light orange bands, with scores near 0 or slightly negative, include Mexico, Brazil, India, and China. Darker orange and red hues, signifying scores between -2 and -4 where abuses are relatively widespread, appear across much of the Middle East, including Saudi Arabia, Iran, and Yemen, as well as Algeria, Libya, Sudan, Myanmar, and Venezuela. Russia and Ukraine fall into the -1 to -2 range. Large areas of central Africa and parts of Asia are shaded grey, indicating no data is available for those regions. The geographic distribution shows the highest scores concentrated in northern Europe, North America, and Oceania, while the lowest scores cluster in the Middle East, North Africa, and parts of South Asia.
